description:
Track the owner/manual confirmations that should not be guessed or silently automated for the current single-account AWS launch setup.

**Scope**
- Confirm and document the single-account launch decision: account `329425487030` is staging now and may host production-stage resources later, separated by CDK stage/resource names until an account split is warranted.
- Confirm account-wide MFA posture for developer/admin users in IAM Identity Center.
- Confirm no long-lived developer IAM user access keys are active or required.
- Choose the security findings owner/contact path for CloudTrail/GuardDuty alerts.
- Decide the GitHub repository and branch/environment/workflow restrictions for OIDC deploy role trust.
- Populate live MDI, Stripe, and app-signing secret values in AWS Secrets Manager only; do not paste or commit secret values.

**Acceptance**
- `docs/runbooks/aws-account-baseline.md` records the single-account launch exception and all owner-provided non-secret values.
- The staging handoff no longer has TODOs for MFA/key posture, security findings contact, or GitHub trust restrictions once decisions are made.
- Secrets Manager containers are populated in AWS, and docs record only secret ARNs/names, never secret values.
- ISS-002 is resolved only after these manual confirmations and the related implementation tickets are complete.
title: Owner manual AWS baseline confirmations for single-account launch
